title: ampelocissus heracleifolia
intro: Ampelocissus heracleifolia, also known as the Chinese grape creeper or wild grape, is a type of perennial vine native to China and Southeast Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and relieving pain. Herbal preparations of Ampelocissus heracleifolia include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
